joflux
GitHub Releases Homebrew

GitHub to Forgejo migration workflow

joflux

Export a GitHub organization, start Forgejo or Codeberg imports, monitor progress, verify the target repositories, and archive the originals when the move is complete.

Install Docs
  1. exportinventory GitHub repos
  2. migratestart Forgejo imports
  3. monitorwatch queued jobs
  4. verifyrecord target metadata
  5. archiveclose the GitHub originals
Latest release Not published yet
Commit main
Published Pending first release

Install

Choose the path that fits the run.

brew tap netspeedy/joflux
brew install joflux
Open tap 
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install .
Open releases 
export JOFLUX_GITHUB_TOKEN="ghp_..."
export JOFLUX_FORGEJO_TOKEN="forgejo_..."
Configuration docs

What it keeps tidy

A migration with a paper trail.

Inventory first

Review the exported repository list before any target-side import starts.

Forgejo-compatible

Targets Codeberg and self-hosted Forgejo instances through the migration API.

JSON state

Each step writes readable state files for retries, reporting, and handoff.

joflux Releases Homebrew Pending first release
